text: Plugins for Excel, PowerPoint, Word, … coming soon https://t.co/BvT3aEO6Zk
summary: Plugins for Excel, PowerPoint, Word, … coming soon
ai_summary: Elon Musk announced that plugins for Microsoft Office applications (Excel, PowerPoint, Word) are coming soon, likely referring to ChatGPT/OpenAI integration capabilities. This signals expansion of AI tool accessibility into enterprise productivity software that millions of business users rely on daily, representing a significant distribution and monetization strategy for AI-powered features in core business applications.
embed_html:
Plugins for Excel, PowerPoint, Word, … coming soon https://t.co/BvT3aEO6Zk

— Elon Musk (@elonmusk) April 20, 2026

ai_summary: Beffjezos raises concerns about competitive positioning in AI development, arguing that xAI's Grok must surpass Anthropic's Claude to prevent regulatory capture and centralized AI control. Elon's response confirms xAI is actively developing Grok to achieve greater general intelligence capabilities beyond its current deployment on X, signaling a direct business strategy to compete in the large language model market against established players like OpenAI and Anthropic.

summary: @TheHumanoidHub @GoingBallistic5 We already changed the design. This one didn’t actually work.

parent.text: Scott Walter (@GoingBallistic5) explains Tesla Optimus' innovative rolling contact finger joints from the newly published patents.
parent.name: The Humanoid Hub
parent.embed_html:
Scott Walter (@GoingBallistic5) explains Tesla Optimus' innovative rolling contact finger joints from the newly published patents. pic.twitter.com/x81qTf9cFg

— The Humanoid Hub (@TheHumanoidHub) April 19, 2026

ai_summary: Scott Walter analyzed Tesla Optimus' rolling contact finger joints from newly published patents, but Elon Musk clarified that Tesla has already iterated beyond this design because the original mechanism didn't work as intended. This illustrates a critical product development lesson: published patents don't always reflect current engineering solutions, and successful robotics development requires rapid iteration and willingness to abandon approaches that fail in practice—a key insight for operators building hardware products.
embed_html:
We already changed the design. This one didn’t actually work.

— Elon Musk (@elonmusk) April 19, 2026
